name: Booking 1.0.26-SNAPSHOT Jackson/DW Version Conflict
description: Diagnosing build failures in booking after upgrading commons to 1.0.26-SNAPSHOT. Jackson version mismatch between DW 2.1.1 (mercury-services) and DW 5.0.1 (commons).

entries.summary: Root cause: Dropwizard version divergence between mercury-services (DW 2.1.1 → Jackson 2.13.4) and commons 1.0.26-SNAPSHOT (DW 5.0.1 → Jackson 2.21.0)
entries.detail:
The installed commons-1.0.26-SNAPSHOT.pom (10KB, flatten fix applied) directly declares DW 5.0.1 modules and jackson-dataformat-csv:2.21.0. These transitively bring Jackson 2.21.0 into booking's classpath, conflicting with booking's DW 2.1.1 which expects Jackson 2.13.4.

Error 1: NoSuchField READ_DATE_TIMESTAMPS_AS_NANOSECONDS — this DeserializationFeature was removed in Jackson 2.19+ (moved to DateTimeFeature in jackson-datatype-jsr310). With jackson-databind:2.21.0 on classpath, code referencing this field fails.

Error 2: NoSuchMethod YAMLFactory._createContentReference(Object) — jackson-dataformat-yaml at an older version (2.17.2 from local-elasticsearch) is binary-incompatible with jackson-core:2.21.0 from DW 5.0.1.

Additionally, DW 2.1.1 uses javax.* APIs while DW 5.0.1 uses jakarta.* — this is a fundamental API incompatibility beyond just Jackson.

entries.summary: Root cause confirmed: local-elasticsearch:1.R.01.004 wins Maven nearest-wins for jackson-databind (2.17.2) over commons' 2.21.0, causing a 4-way Jackson version split
entries.detail:
local-elasticsearch:1.R.01.004 (test scope) directly declares jackson-databind:2.17.2 and jackson-dataformat-yaml:2.17.2. These are at depth 2 from booking (booking → local-elasticsearch → jackson-*), while commons → DW 5.0.1 → jackson-*:2.21.0 are at depth 3. Maven nearest-wins picks 2.17.2 for databind and yaml.

Resolved Jackson version split:
- jackson-core: 2.21.0 (from commons)
- jackson-databind: 2.17.2 (from local-elasticsearch, WINS by nearest)
- jackson-annotations: 2.17.0 (from swagger-core)
- jackson-datatype-jsr310: 2.21.0 (from commons)
- jackson-dataformat-yaml: 2.17.2 (from local-elasticsearch, WINS by nearest)
- jackson-dataformat-xml: 2.21.0 (from commons)

This 4-way version split causes binary incompatibilities:
1. jsr310:2.21.0 + databind:2.17.2 → NoSuchField READ_DATE_TIMESTAMPS_AS_NANOSECONDS
2. yaml:2.17.2 + core:2.21.0 → NoSuchMethod YAMLFactory._createContentReference

entries.summary: Jackson classpath ordering fix WORKING - build results: 3,243 tests total, 2 pre-existing DynamoDB failures, 0 Jackson errors
entries.detail:
Fix: Added explicit Jackson deps (jackson-databind, jackson-core, jackson-annotations, jackson-dataformat-yaml) without version (managed by BOM) BEFORE local-elasticsearch in booking/pom.xml. This ensures standalone Jackson jars appear first on classpath, beating the uber-jar's bundled old classes.

Results:
- Surefire JUnit5: 1,969 run, 0 fail, 1 skip
- Surefire TestNG: 983 run, 0 fail, 9 skip  
- Failsafe JUnit5: 142 run, 0 fail
- Failsafe TestNG: 149 run, 2 fail (DynamoDB ConditionalCheckFailed - pre-existing, not Jackson-related)
- Total: 3,243 tests, 2 failures (pre-existing)

Key learnings:
1. test scope for Jackson deps overrides compile-scope transitives from commons - breaks main code compilation
2. jackson-annotations versioning changed in 2.20+ (uses 2.21 not 2.21.0)
3. Removed local-dynamodb dep (stale, booking uses dynamo-integration-test)
4. dynamo-integration-test is 18KB normal jar, no uber-jar issue

entries.summary: Final fix: exclusions on local-elasticsearch + BOM safety net. All 2,952 unit tests pass.
entries.detail:
Switched from explicit Jackson deps to exclusions-only approach. Final booking/pom.xml changes:
1. Jackson BOM in dependencyManagement (safety net for swagger-core, elasticsearch, aws-sdk transitives)
2. Exclusions for all 4 Jackson modules on local-elasticsearch (jackson-databind, jackson-core, jackson-annotations, jackson-dataformat-yaml)
3. Removed stale local-dynamodb dependency
4. No explicit Jackson deps — all comes from commons via DW 5.0.1

Build: 3,243 tests, 0 unit test failures, 2-6 flaky integration failures (pre-existing)
Updated netty-critical.md §10.5-10.7
